title: File names should follow a naming convention
description: File names should comply with a naming conventionAnalyze your codeconsistency-identifiablemaintainabilityCode SmellconventioncertWhy is this an issue?More InfoShared coding conventions allow teams to collaborate effectively. For that reason, file names should conform to a defined standard. This rule raises an issue when the names of analyzed files don’t match the provided regular expression.Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CIDeveloper EditionAvailable Since9.1©2025SonarSource Sàrl.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
// Files with inconsistent naming
MyFile-Name.cpp // Noncompliant: mixed case with dashes
some_FILE.h // Noncompliant: mixed case with underscores
CAMELCase.hpp // Noncompliant: inconsistent capitalization
compliant:
// Files following consistent naming convention
my_file_name.cpp // Compliant: lowercase with underscores
header_file.h // Compliant: consistent lowercase
utility_functions.hpp // Compliant: follows convention

checks.message: File names should follow a naming convention - use lowercase with underscores (e.g., my_file.cpp)
